title: rubus rigidus
intro: Rubus rigidus, also known as the stiff thistle-brush or western thimbleberry, is a species of thimbleberry native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Rubus rigidus include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Rubus rigidus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and reduce the severity of various health conditions.
